lesson_metadata.title: 教育
lesson_metadata.title_localized: 子どもの教育について話し合う

lines.translation: 李哲の両親はメキシコからの移民です。彼と兄は二人ともカリフォルニアで生まれ育ちました。彼の兄はコンピューターサイエンスの博士号を持っていて、ソフトウェアの設計やウェブサイトの管理をしています。李哲の義理の姉は15年前に香港からアメリカに留学し、修士号を取得した後、仕事を見つけてアメリカに定住しました。兄夫婦は結婚して10年になり、とても幸せな生活を送ってきました。しかし、この2年間、娘の教育について意見が食い違うことが多く、時には激しく口論することもありました。ある日、李哲が姪と電話で話していると、リーシャが入ってくるのが見えました。
ああ、大したことじゃないの。田明を探していて、もしかしたらあなたと一緒にいるかと思って。電話を切らせちゃってごめんなさい。
さあ、座って座って。今のは姪からの電話だよ。ちょうど話し終わったところだから。
姪がいるの？今年いくつになるの？
今年でちょうど8歳になったばかりだよ。
勉強のことだよ。姪はまだ小学3年生なのに、義理の姉さんがもう予定を組んでいてね。月曜はピアノ、火曜は絵画、水曜は水泳、木曜はアイススケート。金曜だけ休みで、土曜は中国語学校に行かなきゃいけないんだ。中国語の勉強が好きじゃないから、泣きながら不満を言ってきたんだよ。
そんなにひどいこと言ったの？なんて言ってたの？
子どもだって人間なんだから、自分の趣味や興味があるんだって。親は子どもの選択を尊重すべきだって言ってたよ。
そんなにしっかりしたこと言うなんて、すごいわね！
きっと兄貴から聞いた言葉だろうね。義理の姉さんは一日中あれこれ勉強させてるから。兄貴は子どもにプレッシャーをかけすぎるのは反対なんだ。子どもにとって一番大切なのは、幸せな子ども時代を過ごすことだって考えてるからね。
私の幼少期はすごく幸せだったわ。でも今、中国語の授業を受けるたびに思うの。もし両親が小さい頃に中国語を習わせてくれていたら、今頃もっと楽だっただろうなって。
まさか君が義理の姉さんと同じ意見だとはね！彼女のやり方に賛成なの？
完全に賛成というわけではないけど、理解はできるわ。
多くの親は、息子には龍のように、娘には鳳凰のように立派になってほしいと願うものよね。子どもが小さいうちに一生懸命勉強して、将来素晴らしいキャリアを築けるようにと。でも、子どもにとって将来「龍や鳳凰になる」ことと、幸せな子ども時代を過ごすこと、どちらが良いのか、私には今でもはっきりとは分からないわ。
私の意見としては、どちらも大切だと思うわ。人はもちろん幸せな子ども時代を過ごすべきだけど、大人になったら社会の役に立つ存在であるべきよ。だから、学校でもっと多くの知識を学ぶのが一番良いと思うわ。
でも忘れないで。知識の中には、本から学ぶものだけじゃなくて、社会や人生から学ぶものもあるってことを。
李哲、すっかり哲学者みたいになっちゃって。

example_sentence.cn: 李哲的父母是墨西哥移民,他和他哥哥都是在加州出生、长大的。
example_sentence.py: Lǐ Zhé de fùmǔ shì Mòxīgē yímín, tā hé tā gēge dōu shì zài Jiāzhōu chūshēng, zhǎngdà de.
example_sentence.translation: 李哲の両親はメキシコからの移民です。彼と兄は二人ともカリフォルニアで生まれ育ちました。

example.cn: 李哲的父母是墨西哥移民。
example.py: Lǐ Zhé de fùmǔ shì Mòxīgē yímín.
example.translation: 李哲の両親はメキシコからの移民です。

example_sentence.cn: 李哲的父母是墨西哥移民,他和他哥哥都是在加州出生、长大的。哥哥是电脑博士,设计软件、管理网站。李哲的嫂子十五年前从香港来美国留学,拿到硕士学位以后找到工作,就在美国住了下来。哥哥和嫂子结婚十年了,生活一直很幸福。可是最近两年在女儿的教育问题上,意见常常不同,有时吵得很厉害。一天,李哲正在电话上跟侄女聊天儿,看见丽莎走了进来。
example_sentence.py: Lǐ Zhé de fùmǔ shì Mòxīgē yímín tā hé tā gēge dōu shì zài Jiāzhōu chūshēng zhǎngdà de gēge shì diànnǎo bóshì shèjì ruǎnjiàn guǎnlǐ wǎngzhàn Lǐ Zhé de sǎozi shíwǔ nián qián cóng Xiānggǎng lái Měiguó liúxué nádào shuòshì xuéwèi yǐhòu zhǎodào gōngzuò jiù zài Měiguó zhù le xiàlái gēge hé sǎozi jiéhūn shí nián le shēnghuó yìzhí hěn xìngfú kěshì zuìjìn liǎng nián zài nǚ'ér de jiàoyù wèntí shàng yìjiàn chángcháng bù tóng yǒushí chǎo de hěn lìhai yìtiān Lǐ Zhé zhèngzài diànhuà shàng gēn zhínǚ liáotiānr kànjiàn Lìshā zǒu le jìnlái

example.translation: 李哲の両親はメキシコからの移民です。彼と兄は二人ともカリフォルニアで生まれ育ちました。彼の兄はコンピューターの博士で、ソフトウェアの設計やウェブサイトの管理をしています。李哲の義姉は15年前に香港からアメリカへ留学し、修士号を取得した後、仕事を見つけてアメリカに定住しました。彼の兄夫婦は結婚して10年になり、ずっと幸せな生活を送っていました。しかし、ここ2年ほどは娘の教育について意見が食い違うことが多く、時には激しく口論することもありました。ある日、李哲が姪と電話で話していると、リサが入ってくるのが見えました。

example_sentence.cn: 我侄女才小学三年级,我嫂子就安排她:星期一学钢琴,星期二学画画儿,星期三学游泳,星期四学滑冰,只有星期五休息一天,星期六又叫她去上中文学校。
example_sentence.py: Wǒ zhínǚ cái xiǎoxué sān niánjí, wǒ sǎozi jiù ānpái tā: xīngqīyī xué gāngqín, xīngqī'èr xué huàhuàr, xīngqīsān xué yóuyǒng, xīngqīsì xué huábīng, zhǐyǒu xīngqīwǔ xiūxi yì tiān, xīngqīliù yòu jiào tā qù shàng Zhōngwén xuéxiào.
example_sentence.translation: 姪はまだ小学三年生ですが、義理の姉はもう彼女のために月曜日はピアノ、火曜日は絵画、水曜日は水泳、木曜日はアイススケートと予定を組んでいます。金曜日だけが休みで、土曜日は中国語学校に行かなければなりません。

example.translation: 勉強についてです。姪はまだ小学三年生ですが、義理の姉はもう彼女のために月曜日はピアノ、火曜日は絵画、水曜日は水泳、木曜日はアイススケートと予定を組んでいて、金曜日だけが休みです。土曜日にはまた中国語学校に行かされます。彼女は中国語の勉強が好きではないので、泣きながら私に電話して不平を言いました。

vocabulary.definition: 息子が立派な人物になることを願う、文字通りには「息子が龍になることを願う」という意味です。

example_sentence.cn: 很多父母望子成龙,望女成凤,希望自己的孩子小时候好好儿学习,将来能做出一翻大事业。
example_sentence.py: Hěn duō fùmǔ wàng zǐ chéng lóng, wàng nǚ chéng fèng, xīwàng zìjǐ de háizi xiǎoshíhou hǎohāor xuéxí, jiānglái néng zuòchū yì fān dà shìyè.
example_sentence.translation: 多くの親は、息子には龍のように、娘には鳳凰のように立派になってほしいと願っています。子供たちが幼いうちに一生懸命勉強して、将来素晴らしいキャリアを築けるようにと願っているのです。

historical_usages.definition: to hope that one's son will become successful (lit. to hope that one's son will become a dragon)

example.cn: 很多父母望子成龙,望女成凤,希望自己的孩子小时候好好儿学习,将来能做出一番大事业。
example.py: Hěn duō fùmǔ wàng zǐ chéng lóng, wàng nǚ chéng fèng, xīwàng zìjǐ de háizi xiǎoshíhou hǎohāor xuéxí, jiānglái néng zuòchū yī fān dà shìyè.
example.translation: 多くの親は、息子には龍のように、娘には鳳凰のように立派になってほしいと願っています。子供たちが幼いうちに一生懸命勉強して、将来素晴らしいキャリアを築けるようにと願っているのです。

vocabulary.definition: 娘が立派な人物になることを願う、文字通りには「娘が鳳凰になることを願う」という意味です。

example_sentence.cn: 很多父母望子成龙,望女成凤,希望自己的孩子小时候好好儿学习,将来能做出一翻大事业。

example_sentence.translation: 多くの親は、息子が龍になり娘が鳳凰になることを願っています。子供たちが若いうちに一生懸命勉強して、将来素晴らしいキャリアを築けるようにと願っているのです。

historical_usages.definition: to hope that one's daughter will become successful (lit. to hope that one's daughter will become a phoenix)

example.cn: 很多父母望子成龙,望女成凤,希望自己的孩子小时候好好儿学习,将来能做出一番大事业。

example.translation: 多くの親は、息子が龍になり娘が鳳凰になることを願い、子供たちが若いうちに一生懸命勉強して、将来素晴らしいキャリアを築けるようにと望んでいます。

vocabulary.definition: 種類やタイプを表す量詞

example.cn: 很多父母望子成龙,望女成凤,希望自己的孩子小时候好好儿学习,将来能做出一番大事业。可是到现在我也想不清楚,孩子到底是将来“成龙”“成凤”好,还是有一个快乐的童年好?
example.py: hěn duō fù mǔ wàng zǐ chéng lóng wàng nǚ chéng fèng xī wàng zì jǐ de hái zi xiǎo shí hou hǎo hāor xué xí jiāng lái néng zuò chū yì fān dà shì yè kě shì dào xiàn zài wǒ yě xiǎng bù qīng chu hái zi dào dǐ shì jiāng lái chéng lóng chéng fèng hǎo hái shì yǒu yí gè kuài lè de tóng nián hǎo
example.translation: 多くの親は、息子が龍になり娘が鳳凰になることを願い、子供たちが若いうちに一生懸命勉強して、将来素晴らしいキャリアを築けるようにと望んでいます。しかし、今でも私は、子供が将来「龍」や「鳳凰」になることと、幸せな子供時代を過ごすことのどちらが良いのか、まだよく分かりません。

example_sentence.cn: 很多父母望子成龙,望女成凤,希望自己的孩子小时候好好儿学习,将来能做出一翻大事业。
example_sentence.py: Hěn duō fùmǔ wàng zǐ chéng lóng, wàng nǚ chéng fèng, xīwàng zìjǐ de háizi xiǎoshíhou hǎohāor xuéxí, jiānglái néng zuò chū yī fān dà shìyè.

example.cn: 很多父母望子成龙,望女成凤,希望自己的孩子小时候好好儿学习,将来能做出一番大事业。
example.py: Hěnduō fùmǔ wàng zǐ chéng lóng, wàng nǚ chéng fèng, xīwàng zìjǐ de háizi xiǎoshíhou hǎohāor xuéxí, jiānglái néng zuòchū yī fān dà shìyè.
example.translation: 多くの親は、息子が龍になり娘が鳳凰になることを願い、子供たちが若いうちに一生懸命勉強して、将来素晴らしいキャリアを築けるようにと望んでいます。

example_sentence.cn: 可是你别忘了,有些知识不是从书上学的,而是从社会、从生活中学的。
example_sentence.py: Kěshì nǐ bié wàng le, yǒuxiē zhīshi búshì cóng shū shang xué de, érshì cóng shèhuì, cóng shēnghuó zhōng xué de.
example_sentence.translation: しかし、忘れてはいけません。知識の中には、本からではなく、社会や人生から学ぶものもあるということを。

example.cn: 可是你别忘了,有些知识不是从书上学的,而是从社会、从生活中学的。
example.py: Kěshì nǐ bié wàng le, yǒuxiē zhīshi búshì cóng shū shàng xué de, érshì cóng shèhuì, cóng shēnghuó zhōng xué de.
example.translation: しかし、忘れてはいけません。知識の中には、本からではなく、社会や人生から学ぶものもあるということを。

example_sentence.cn: 一个人当然应该有快乐的童年,但是长大以后也应该对社会有用,所以孩子最好还是在学校多学一些知识。
example_sentence.py: Yí ge rén dāngrán yīnggāi yǒu kuàilè de tóngnián, dànshì zhǎng dà yǐhòu yě yīnggāi duì shèhuì yǒuyòng, suǒyǐ háizi zuìhǎo háishi zài xuéxiào duō xué yìxiē zhīshi.
example_sentence.translation: 人は当然、幸せな子供時代を過ごすべきですが、大人になったら社会の役に立つべきでもあります。だから、子供たちは学校でもっと多くの知識を学ぶのが一番です。

example.cn: 所以孩子最好还是在学校多学一些知识。
example.py: Suǒyǐ háizi zuìhǎo háishì zài xuéxiào duō xué yīxiē zhīshi.
example.translation: だから、子供たちは学校でもっと多くの知識を学ぶのが一番です。

example_sentence.cn: 李哲的父母是墨西哥移民,他和他哥哥都是在加州出生、长大的。哥哥是电脑博士,设计软件、管理网站。李哲的嫂子十五年前从香港来美国留学,拿到硕士学位以后找到工作,就在美国住了下来。哥哥和嫂子结婚十年了,生活一直很幸福。可是最近两年在女儿的教育问题上,意见常常不同,有时吵得很厉害。一天,李哲正在电话上跟侄女聊天儿,看见丽莎走了进来。
example_sentence.py: Lǐ Zhé de fùmǔ shì Mòxīgē yímín tā hé tā gēge dōu shì zài Jiāzhōu chūshēng zhǎngdà de gēge shì diànnǎo bóshì shèjì ruǎnjiàn guǎnlǐ wǎngzhàn Lǐ Zhé de sǎozi shíwǔ nián qián cóng Xiānggǎng lái Měiguó liúxué nádào shuòshì xuéwèi yǐhòu zhǎodào gōngzuò jiù zài Měiguó zhù le xiàlái gēge hé sǎozi jiéhūn shí nián le shēnghuó yìzhí hěn xìngfú kěshì zuìjìn liǎng nián zài nǚ'ér de jiàoyù wèntí shàng yìjiàn chángcháng bù tóng yǒushí chǎo de hěn lìhai yìtiān Lǐ Zhé zhèngzài diànhuà shàng gēn zhínǚ liáotiānr kànjiàn Lìshā zǒu le jìnlái
example_sentence.translation: 李哲の両親はメキシコからの移民です。彼と兄は二人ともカリフォルニアで生まれ育ちました。彼の兄はコンピューターサイエンスの博士号を持っていて、ソフトウェアを設計し、ウェブサイトを管理しています。李哲の義理の姉は15年前に香港からアメリカに留学し、修士号を取得した後、仕事を見つけてアメリカに定住しました。彼の兄夫婦は結婚して10年になり、とても幸せな生活を送ってきました。しかし、この2年間、彼らは娘の教育問題について意見が食い違うことが多く、時には激しく口論することもありました。ある日、李哲が姪と電話で話していると、リーシャが入ってくるのが見えました。

example.cn: 李哲的父母是墨西哥移民,他和他哥哥都是在加州出生、长大的。哥哥是电脑博士,设计软件、管理网站。李哲的嫂子十五年前从香港来美国留学,拿到硕士学位以后找到工作,就在美国住了下来。哥哥和嫂子结婚十年了,生活一直很幸福。可是最近两年在女儿的教育问题上,意见常常不同,有时吵得很厉害。一天,李哲正在电话上跟侄女聊天儿,看见丽莎走了进来。
example.py: Lǐ Zhé de fùmǔ shì Mòxīgē yímín tā hé tā gēge dōu shì zài Jiāzhōu chūshēng zhǎngdà de gēge shì diànnǎo bóshì shèjì ruǎnjiàn guǎnlǐ wǎngzhàn Lǐ Zhé de sǎozi shíwǔ nián qián cóng Xiānggǎng lái Měiguó liúxué nádào shuòshì xuéwèi yǐhòu zhǎodào gōngzuò jiù zài Měiguó zhù le xiàlai gēge hé sǎozi jiéhūn shí nián le shēnghuó yìzhí hěn xìngfú kěshì zuìjìn liǎng nián zài nǚ'ér de jiàoyù wèntí shàng yìjiàn chángcháng bùtóng yǒushí chǎo de hěn lìhai yìtiān Lǐ Zhé zhèngzài diànhuà shàng gēn zhínǚ liáotiānr kànjiàn Lìshā zǒu le jìnlái
example.translation: 李哲の両親はメキシコからの移民です。彼と兄は二人ともカリフォルニアで生まれ育ちました。彼の兄はコンピューターの博士号を持っていて、ソフトウェアを設計し、ウェブサイトを管理しています。李哲の義理の姉は15年前に香港からアメリカに留学しました。修士号を取得した後、仕事を見つけてアメリカに定住しました。彼の兄夫婦は結婚して10年になり、ずっと幸せな生活を送ってきました。しかし、この2年間、彼らは娘の教育問題について意見が食い違うことが多く、時には激しく口論することもありました。ある日、李哲が姪と電話で話していると、リーサが入ってくるのが見えました。

language_notes.label: a
language_notes.explanation_en: 学士号は[zh:xuéshì xuéwèi]（学士学位）と言います。[zh:bóshì xuéwèi]（博士学位）と[zh:shuòshì xuéwèi]（修士学位）は、それぞれ博士号と修士号を表す言葉です。

language_notes.label: b
language_notes.explanation_en: ピアノは[zh:gāngqín]（鋼琴）と言います（文字通り「鋼の楽器」）。バイオリンは[zh:xiǎotíqín]（小提琴）と言います（文字通り「小さい手持ちの楽器」）。では、[zh:zhōngtíqín]（中提琴）と[zh:dàtíqín]（大提琴）が何だか分かりますか？

language_notes.label: c
language_notes.explanation_en: ここで[zh:huìshuōhuà]（会说话）は「とても口達者である」という意味です。

language_notes.label: d
language_notes.explanation_en: [zh:rènwéi]（认为）は[zh:duì…láishuō]（对…来说）とは異なります。[zh:duì…láishuō]（对…来说）が話し手の意見しか伝えられないのに対し、[zh:rènwéi]（认为）は話し手の意見も、話題になっている人の意見も表現できます。

language_notes.label: e
language_notes.explanation_en: [zh:jiā]（家）は、特定の分野の専門家や確立された学者を意味する接尾辞として使われます。例えば、[zh:huàjiā]（画家）、[zhéxuéjiā]（哲学家）、[wénxuéjiā]（文学家）、[lìshǐxuéjiā]（歴史学家）、[huàxuéjiā]（化学家）などです。謙遜から、多くの人は自分自身を指すのにこの接尾辞を使うのを避けます。自分を[zh:huàjiā]（画家）と呼ぶのは、少し大げさに聞こえることがあります。そのため、人々は代わりに自分のしていることを言うことが多いです。例えば、[zh:wǒ huà huàr]（我画画儿）や[zh:wǒ shì huà huàr de]（我是画画儿的）のように、「私は絵を描く人です」と表現します。

grammar_sections.title: 数字の前の副詞[zh:cái]（才）
grammar_sections.explanation_en: [zh:cái]（才）は、少ない数量を表すために数字の前に使われます。

examples.hanzi: 我每天上网才三个小时，时间不长。
examples.translation: 私は一日にたった3時間しかネットをしません。それはあまり長い時間ではありません。

examples.hanzi: 现在才六点，球赛八点开始，不用这么早去。
examples.translation: まだ6時だよ。野球の試合は8時に始まるんだから、そんなに早く行く必要ないよ。

examples.hanzi: 你今年才二十岁，就想结婚？
examples.translation: まだ20歳なのに、もう結婚したいの？

examples.hanzi: 我大哥二十岁就念完博士了，我二十岁才上大学。
examples.translation: 兄はまだ20歳で博士号を取ったんだ。僕は20歳になってやっと大学に入ったのに。

usage_notes: 才[zh:cái]は、数字や量詞の後、または動詞の前に使われると、以前学んだように、遅さや時間の経過を表します。

grammar_sections.title: 述語となる形容詞
grammar_sections.explanation_en: 形容詞が述語として使われる場合、文の主語は「名詞＋述語」のフレーズになることがあります。

examples.hanzi: 你说，我们寒假去哪儿旅行好？
examples.pinyin: Nǐ shuō, wǒmen hánjià qù nǎr lǚxíng hǎo?
examples.translation: ねえ、冬休みはどこに旅行に行こうか？

examples.hanzi: 今天晚上有一个生日舞会，我不知道穿什么衣服合适。
examples.pinyin: Jīntiān wǎnshang yǒu yí ge shēngrì wǔhuì, wǒ bù zhīdào chuān shénme yīfu héshì.
examples.translation: 今夜は誕生日パーティーがあるんだけど、何を着ていけばいいか分からないな。

examples.hanzi: 你现在读书最重要，找工作的事儿不用着急。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ xiànzài dúshū zuì zhòngyào, zhǎo gōngzuò de shìr búyòng zhāojí.
examples.translation: 君にとって今一番大切なのは勉強だよ。仕事を探すのは急がなくてもいいんだ。

grammar_sections.title: 不是X, 而是Y [zh:bú shì X, ér shì Y]
grammar_sections.explanation_en: 「不是X, 而是Y [zh:bú shì X, ér shì Y]」というパターンは、ある発言や状況に対して、話し手がXを否定し、Yを強く肯定したいときに使われます。

patterns.pattern_pinyin: bú shì X, ér shì Y

examples.hanzi: 丽莎, 你知道有些知识不是从书上学的, 而是从社会、从生活中学的。
examples.pinyin: Lìshā, nǐ zhīdào yǒuxiē zhīshi bú shì cóng shū shang xué de, ér shì cóng shèhuì, cóng shēnghuó zhōng xué de.
examples.translation: リーシャ、学校や本だけでは学べない知識もあるって知ってるでしょ。むしろ、それは社会や人生から学ぶものなのよ。

examples.hanzi: 我不是不希望孩子将来有一番大事业, 而是不想给他太大的压力。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ bú shì bù xīwàng háizi jiānglái yǒu yì fān dà shìyè, ér shì bùxiǎng gěi tā tài dà de yālì.
examples.translation: 将来、子どもに素晴らしいキャリアを築いてほしくないわけじゃないの。ただ、あまりプレッシャーをかけたくないだけなのよ。

examples.hanzi: 你这样做不是为他好, 而是给他找麻烦。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ zhèyàng zuò bú shì wèi tā hǎo, ér shì gěi tā zhǎo máfan.
examples.translation: あなたのしていることは彼のためにならないどころか、かえって彼に迷惑をかけることになるでしょう。

examples.hanzi:
Q: 你不叫我买这件衬衫, 是怕我的钱不够, 跟你借钱吗?
A: 你想错了, 我不是怕你跟我借钱, 而是觉得这件衬衫质量不好, 你不应该买。
examples.pinyin:
Q: Nǐ bù jiào wǒ mǎi zhè jiàn chènshān, shì pà wǒ de qián bùgòu, gēn nǐ jièqián ma?
A: Nǐ xiǎng cuò le, wǒ bú shì pà nǐ gēn wǒ jièqián, ér shì juéde zhè jiàn chènshān zhìliàng bù hǎo, nǐ bù yīnggāi mǎi.
examples.translation:
Q: このシャツを買ってほしくないのは、私がお金が足りなくてあなたから借りるのを恐れているから？
A: 違うよ。君がお金を借りるのを恐れているわけじゃない。むしろ、このシャツの質があまり良くないと思うんだ。買わない方がいいよ。

examples.hanzi:
Q: 刚才找你的人是你的女朋友吧?
A: 别乱说, 她不是我的女朋友, 而是我的嫂子。
examples.pinyin:
Q: Gāngcái zhǎo nǐ de rén shì nǐ de nǚpéngyou ba?
A: Bié luàn shuō, tā bú shì wǒ de nǚpéngyou, ér shì wǒ de sǎozi.
examples.translation:
Q: さっきあなたを訪ねてきた人、彼女だったの？
A: 馬鹿なこと言わないでくれ。彼女は僕の彼女じゃなくて、義理の姉さんだよ。

context_examples.cn: 李哲的父母是墨西哥移民,他和他哥哥都是在加州出生、长大的。
context_examples.py: Lǐ Zhé de fùmǔ shì Mòxīgē yímín, tā hé tā gēge dōu shì zài Jiāzhōu chūshēng, zhǎngdà de.
context_examples.translation: リー・ジャーの両親はメキシコからの移民です。彼と兄は二人ともカリフォルニアで生まれ育ちました。

context_examples.cn: 李哲的父母是墨西哥移民,他和他哥哥都是在加州出生、长大的。哥哥是电脑博士,设计软件、管理网站。李哲的嫂子十五年前从香港来美国留学,拿到硕士学位以后找到工作,就在美国住了下来。哥哥和嫂子结婚十年了,生活一直很幸福。可是最近两年在女儿的教育问题上,意见常常不同,有时吵得很厉害。一天,李哲正在电话上跟侄女聊天儿,看见丽莎走了进来。

context_examples.translation: リー・ジャーの両親はメキシコからの移民です。彼と兄は二人ともカリフォルニアで生まれ育ちました。彼の兄はコンピューターサイエンスの博士号を持っていて、ソフトウェアを設計したりウェブサイトを管理したりしています。リー・ジャーの義理の姉は15年前に香港からアメリカに留学し、修士号を取得した後、仕事を見つけてアメリカに定住しました。兄夫婦は結婚して10年になり、とても幸せな生活を送っていましたが、ここ2年ほどは娘の教育について意見が食い違うことが多く、激しく口論することもありました。ある日、リー・ジャーが姪と電話で話していると、リーシャが入ってくるのが見えました。

context_examples.cn: 我侄女才小学三年级,我嫂子就安排她:星期一学钢琴,星期二学画画儿,星期三学游泳,星期四学滑冰,只有星期五休息一天,星期六又叫她去上中文学校。
context_examples.py: Wǒ zhínǚ cái xiǎoxué sān niánjí, wǒ sǎozi jiù ānpái tā: xīngqīyī xué gāngqín, xīngqī'èr xué huàhuàr, xīngqīsān xué yóuyǒng, xīngqīsì xué huábīng, zhǐyǒu xīngqīwǔ xiūxi yì tiān, xīngqīliù yòu jiào tā qù shàng Zhōngwén xuéxiào.
context_examples.translation: 私の姪はまだ小学3年生なのに、義姉はもう彼女のために色々な習い事をさせています。月曜日はピアノ、火曜日は絵画、水曜日は水泳、木曜日はアイススケート。金曜日だけが休みで、土曜日には中国語学校に行かなければなりません。

context_examples.cn: 她不喜欢学中文,所以就打电话到我这儿来,一边哭一边抱怨。
context_examples.py: Tā bù xǐhuan xué Zhōngwén, suǒyǐ jiù dǎ diànhuà dào wǒ zhèr lái, yìbiān kū yìbiān bàoyuàn.
context_examples.translation: 彼女は中国語の勉強が好きじゃないので、泣きながら不平を言いに電話をかけてきました。

context_examples.cn: 父母应该尊重孩子自己的选择。
context_examples.py: Fùmǔ yīnggāi zūnzhòng háizi zìjǐ de xuǎnzé.
context_examples.translation: 親は子供の選択を尊重すべきです。

context_examples.cn: 父母应该尊重孩子自己的选择。

context_examples.translation: 親は子供の選択を尊重すべきです。

context_examples.cn: 这些话一定是跟我哥哥学的。我嫂子整天让她学这学那,我哥哥反对给孩子太大的压力,他认为对孩子来说,最重要的是有一个快乐的童年。
context_examples.py: zhè xiē huà yí dìng shì gēn wǒ gē ge xué de wǒ sǎo zi zhěng tiān ràng tā xué zhè xué nà wǒ gē ge fǎn duì gěi hái zi tài dà de yā lì tā rèn wéi duì hái zi lái shuō zuì zhòng yào de shì yǒu yí gè kuài lè de tóng nián
context_examples.translation: 彼女はきっと、これらの言葉を兄から学んだのでしょう。義姉は一日中、あれこれと勉強させています。兄は子供に過度なプレッシャーをかけることに反対しています。彼は、子供にとって最も大切なのは、幸せな子供時代を送ることだと考えています。

context_examples.cn: 他认为对孩子来说,最重要的是有一个快乐的童年。
context_examples.py: Tā rènwéi duì háizi lái shuō, zuì zhòngyào de shì yǒu yí ge kuàilè de tóngnián.
context_examples.translation: 彼は、子供にとって最も大切なのは、幸せな子供時代を送ることだと考えています。

context_examples.cn: 我的童年很快乐。
context_examples.py: Wǒ de tóngnián hěn kuàilè.
context_examples.translation: 私の子供時代はとても幸せでした。

context_examples.cn: 没想到你跟我嫂子的看法一样!你同意我嫂子的做法?
context_examples.py: méi xiǎngdào nǐ gēn wǒ sǎozi de kànfǎ yíyàng nǐ tóngyì wǒ sǎozi de zuòfǎ
context_examples.translation: 義姉と同じ考えだとは思いませんでした！彼女のやり方に賛成ですか？

context_examples.cn: 你同意我嫂子的做法?
context_examples.py: Nǐ tóngyì wǒ sǎozi de zuòfǎ?
context_examples.translation: 彼女のやり方に賛成ですか？

standard_answers: 完全に、全く；完全な、全体の

context_examples.cn: 我不完全同意,但我能理解。
context_examples.py: Wǒ bù wánquán tóngyì, dàn wǒ néng lǐjiě.
context_examples.translation: 全く賛成というわけではありませんが、理解はできます。

context_examples.cn: 我不完全同意,但我能理解。
context_examples.py: Wǒ bù wánquán tóngyì, dàn wǒ néng lǐjiě.
context_examples.translation: 全く賛成というわけではありませんが、理解はできます。

standard_answers: 息子が成功することを願う（文字通りには「息子が龍になることを願う」） [zh:望子成龙]

context_examples.cn: 很多父母望子成龙,望女成凤,希望自己的孩子小时候好好儿学习,将来能做出一翻大事业。
context_examples.py: Hěn duō fùmǔ wàng zǐ chéng lóng, wàng nǚ chéng fèng, xīwàng zìjǐ de háizi xiǎoshíhou hǎohāor xuéxí, jiānglái néng zuòchū yì fān dà shìyè.
context_examples.translation: 多くの親は、息子には龍のように、娘には鳳凰のように成功してほしいと願っています。子供たちが幼いうちに一生懸命勉強して、将来素晴らしいキャリアを築けるようにと願っているのです。

context_examples.cn: 可是你别忘了,有些知识不是从书上学的,而是从社会、从生活中学的。
context_examples.py: Kěshì nǐ bié wàng le, yǒuxiē zhīshi búshì cóng shū shang xué de, érshì cóng shèhuì, cóng shēnghuó zhōng xué de.
context_examples.translation: でも忘れてはいけません。知識の中には、本からではなく、社会や人生から学ぶものもあるのです。

context_examples.cn: 一个人当然应该有快乐的童年,但是长大以后也应该对社会有用,所以孩子最好还是在学校多学一些知识。
context_examples.py: Yí ge rén dāngrán yīnggāi yǒu kuàilè de tóngnián, dànshì zhǎng dà yǐhòu yě yīnggāi duì shèhuì yǒuyòng, suǒyǐ háizi zuìhǎo háishi zài xuéxiào duō xué yìxiē zhīshi.
context_examples.translation: 人は当然、幸せな子供時代を送るべきですが、大人になったら社会の役に立つべきです。だから、子供たちは学校でもっと多くの知識を学ぶのが一番です。

context_examples.cn: 李哲的父母是墨西哥移民,他和他哥哥都是在加州出生、长大的。哥哥是电脑博士,设计软件、管理网站。李哲的嫂子十五年前从香港来美国留学,拿到硕士学位以后找到工作,就在美国住了下来。哥哥和嫂子结婚十年了,生活一直很幸福。可是最近两年在女儿的教育问题上,意见常常不同,有时吵得很厉害。一天,李哲正在电话上跟侄女聊天儿,看见丽莎走了进来。
context_examples.py: Lǐ Zhé de fùmǔ shì Mòxīgē yímín tā hé tā gēge dōu shì zài Jiāzhōu chūshēng zhǎngdà de gēge shì diànnǎo bóshì shèjì ruǎnjiàn guǎnlǐ wǎngzhàn Lǐ Zhé de sǎozi shíwǔ nián qián cóng Xiānggǎng lái Měiguó liúxué nádào shuòshì xuéwèi yǐhòu zhǎodào gōngzuò jiù zài Měiguó zhù le xiàlái gēge hé sǎozi jiéhūn shí nián le shēnghuó yìzhí hěn xìngfú kěshì zuìjìn liǎng nián zài nǚ'ér de jiàoyù wèntí shàng yìjiàn chángcháng bù tóng yǒushí chǎo de hěn lìhai yìtiān Lǐ Zhé zhèngzài diànhuà shàng gēn zhínǚ liáotiānr kànjiàn Lìshā zǒu le jìnlái
context_examples.translation: 李哲の両親はメキシコからの移民です。彼と兄は二人ともカリフォルニアで生まれ育ちました。彼の兄はコンピューターサイエンスの博士号を持っていて、ソフトウェアの設計やウェブサイトの管理をしています。李哲の義姉は15年前に香港からアメリカに留学し、修士号を取得した後、仕事を見つけてアメリカに定住しました。兄と義姉は結婚して10年になり、とても幸せな生活を送っています。しかし、この2年間、娘の教育問題に関して意見が食い違うことが多く、時には激しく口論することもありました。ある日、李哲が姪と電話で話していると、麗莎が入ってくるのが見えました。

context_examples.cn: 李哲的父母是墨西哥移民,他和他哥哥都是在加州出生、长大的。哥哥是电脑博士,设计软件、管理网站。李哲的嫂子十五年前从香港来美国留学,拿到硕士学位以后找到工作,就在美国住了下来。哥哥和嫂子结婚十年了,生活一直很幸福。可是最近两年在女儿的教育问题上,意见常常不同,有时吵得很厉害。一天,李哲正在电话上跟侄女聊天儿,看见丽莎走了进来。

context_examples.translation: 李哲の両親はメキシコからの移民です。彼と兄は二人ともカリフォルニアで生まれ育ちました。彼の兄はコンピューターサイエンスの博士号を持っていて、ソフトウェアを設計したりウェブサイトを管理したりしています。李哲の義理の姉は15年前に香港からアメリカに留学し、修士号を取得した後、仕事を見つけてアメリカに定住しました。兄夫婦は結婚して10年になり、とても幸せな生活を送っています。しかし、この2年間、娘の教育問題について意見が食い違うことが多く、時には激しく口論することもありました。ある日、李哲が姪と電話で話していると、リーシャが入ってくるのが見えました。

character_definitions.appearance_description_en: An 8-year-old girl, appears tired and sad in flashbacks.

character_definitions.name: 父母
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A Mexican couple, seen in a vintage photograph from the 1980s.

hanzi: 李哲的父母是墨西哥移民,他和他哥哥都是在加州出生、长大的。哥哥是电脑博士,设计软件、管理网站。李哲的嫂子十五年前从香港来美国留学,拿到硕士学位以后找到工作,就在美国住了下来。哥哥和嫂子结婚十年了,生活一直很幸福。可是最近两年在女儿的教育问题上,意见常常不同,有时吵得很厉害。一天,李哲正在电话上跟侄女聊天儿,看见丽莎走了进来。
pinyin: Lǐ Zhé de fùmǔ shì Mòxīgē yímín tā hé tā gēge dōu shì zài Jiāzhōu chūshēng zhǎngdà de gēge shì diànnǎo bóshì shèjì ruǎnjiàn guǎnlǐ wǎngzhàn Lǐ Zhé de sǎozi shíwǔ nián qián cóng Xiānggǎng lái Měiguó liúxué nádào shuòshì xuéwèi yǐhòu zhǎodào gōngzuò jiù zài Měiguó zhù le xiàlái gēge hé sǎozi jiéhūn shí nián le shēnghuó yìzhí hěn xìngfú kěshì zuìjìn liǎng nián zài nǚ'ér de jiàoyù wèntí shàng yìjiàn chángcháng bù tóng yǒushí chǎo de hěn lìhai yìtiān Lǐ Zhé zhèngzài diànhuà shàng gēn zhínǚ liáotiānr kànjiàn Lìshā zǒu le jìnlái
translation: Li Zhe's parents are Mexican immigrants. He and his older brother were both born and raised in California. His brother is a computer science Ph.D. who designs software and manages websites. Li Zhe's sister-in-law came to the U.S. from Hong Kong to study fifteen years ago, and after getting her master's degree, she found a job and settled down in America. His brother and sister-in-law have been married for ten years and have had a very happy life. However, in the last two years, they have often had different opinions on the issue of their daughter's education, sometimes arguing fiercely. One day, Li Zhe was chatting with his niece on the phone when he saw Lisha walk in.

scenes.scene_goal: 通过旁白和蒙太奇画面，快速介绍李哲的家庭背景、哥嫂的矛盾，并引出当前场景。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 一系列快速剪辑的画面：一张老旧的墨西哥夫妇合影；两个男孩在加州阳光下玩耍；哥哥在电脑前工作；嫂子穿着硕士服毕业；两人的婚纱照；然后是两人激烈争吵的画面。最后镜头切到李哲家的客厅，他正温柔地打着电话，丽莎从门口走了进来。
scenes.setting_en: Montage of various locations (old photos, California park, office, university, home), ending in Li Zhe's living room.
scenes.camera_movement: Dynamic quick cuts for the montage, ending on a static shot.
scenes.shot_type: Various, ending in a medium shot.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Narrated montage: 1. Vintage photo of a smiling Mexican couple. 2. Two young boys playing under the California sun. 3. A man in his 30s coding in a modern office. 4. A woman in her 30s in a graduation gown. 5. Their wedding photo. 6. The couple arguing heatedly. 7. The scene settles on Li Zhe in his apartment, talking on the phone with a gentle expression, as Lisha walks in.

voice_over.hanzi: 李哲的父母是墨西哥移民,他和他哥哥都是在加州出生、长大的。哥哥是电脑博士,设计软件、管理网站。李哲的嫂子十五年前从香港来美国留学,拿到硕士学位以后找到工作,就在美国住了下来。哥哥和嫂子结婚十年了,生活一直很幸福。可是最近两年在女儿的教育问题上,意见常常不同,有时吵得很厉害。一天,李哲正在电话上跟侄女聊天儿,看见丽莎走了进来。

Li Zhe's sister-in-law came to the U.S. from Hong Kong to study fifteen years ago, and after getting her master's degree, she found a job and settled down in America. scenes.scene_goal: 李哲结束通话，开始与丽莎交谈。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头紧接上一幕。李哲看到丽莎后，迅速挂断电话，然后友好地看着她，开口询问。
scenes.setting_en: Li Zhe's modern, cozy living room.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Continuing from the previous scene, Li Zhe hangs up his phone and looks at Lisha, who has just entered his living room. He smiles and asks her a question.

hanzi: 没什么事儿,我找天明,以为他在你这儿呢。对不起,害得你把电话挂了。
pinyin: méi shénme shìr wǒ zhǎo Tiānmíng yǐwéi tā zài nǐ zhèr ne duìbuqǐ hàide nǐ bǎ diànhuà guà le
translation: Oh, nothing important. I was looking for Tianming and thought he might be here with you. Sorry to make you hang up the phone.

scenes.scene_goal: 丽莎解释来意并为打扰道歉。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 丽莎略带歉意地笑了笑，解释说她以为天明在这里，并为自己害他挂断电话而道歉。
scenes.setting_en: Li Zhe's modern, cozy living room.
scenes.shot_type: Medium shot, focusing on Lisha's expression.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Lisha gives a small, apologetic smile. She explains she was looking for Tianming and says sorry for making him hang up his call.

voice_over.speaker: 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 没什么事儿,我找天明,以为他在你这儿呢。对不起,害得你把电话挂了。
voice_over.pinyin: méi shénme shìr wǒ zhǎo Tiānmíng yǐwéi tā zài nǐ zhèr ne duìbuqǐ hàide nǐ bǎ diànhuà guà le
voice_over.translation: Oh, nothing important. I was looking for Tianming and thought he might be here with you. Sorry to make you hang up the phone.

hanzi: 坐,坐,坐。刚才是我侄女给我打电话,我们正好说完了。
pinyin: zuò zuò zuò gāngcái shì wǒ zhínǚ gěi wǒ dǎ diànhuà wǒmen zhènghǎo shuō wán le
translation: Have a seat, have a seat. That was my niece calling me. We just finished talking.

scenes.scene_goal: 李哲请丽莎坐下，并解释通话已经结束。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 李哲热情地招呼丽莎坐下，并解释说刚才打电话的是他的侄女，他们正好说完了，让她不必在意。
scenes.setting_en: Li Zhe's modern, cozy living room.
scenes.camera_movement: Camera follows as Lisha moves to sit down.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Li Zhe gestures warmly towards the sofa, inviting Lisha to sit. He smiles and explains, 'Have a seat. That was my niece calling me. We just finished talking.' Lisha sits down.

voice_over.speaker: 李哲
voice_over.hanzi: 坐,坐,坐。刚才是我侄女给我打电话,我们正好说完了。
voice_over.pinyin: zuò zuò zuò gāngcái shì wǒ zhínǚ gěi wǒ dǎ diànhuà wǒmen zhènghǎo shuō wán le
voice_over.translation: Have a seat, have a seat. That was my niece calling me. We just finished talking.

pinyin: nǐ yǒu zhí nǚ jīn nián duō dà le
translation: You have a niece? How old is she this year?

scenes.scene_goal: 丽莎对李哲有侄女感到好奇，并询问其年龄。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 丽莎坐下后，脸上露出感兴趣的表情，她问李哲关于他侄女的情况，好奇她今年多大了。
scenes.setting_en: Li Zhe's modern, cozy living room.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Now sitting on the sofa, Lisha looks at Li Zhe with curiosity and asks about his niece's age.

voice_over.speaker: 丽莎
voice_over.pinyin: nǐ yǒu zhí nǚ jīn nián duō dà le
voice_over.translation: You have a niece? How old is she this year?

translation: She just turned eight this year.

scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头切换到李哲，他简单地回答了侄女的年龄。
scenes.setting_en: Li Zhe's modern, cozy living room.
scenes.video_prompt_en: The camera cuts to Li Zhe, who replies matter-of-factly that his niece is eight.

voice_over.speaker: 李哲
voice_over.translation: She just turned eight this year.

hanzi: 她给你打电话有什么事啊?
pinyin: tā gěi nǐ dǎ diàn huà yǒu shén me shì a
translation: What did she call you about?

scenes.scene_goal: 丽莎追问侄女打电话的原因。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头切回丽莎，她身体微微前倾，更显好奇，追问侄女打电话来的具体原因。
scenes.setting_en: Li Zhe's modern, cozy living room.
scenes.video_prompt_en: The camera cuts back to Lisha. She leans forward slightly, her curiosity piqued, and asks why his niece called him.

voice_over.speaker: 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 她给你打电话有什么事啊?
voice_over.pinyin: tā gěi nǐ dǎ diàn huà yǒu shén me shì a
voice_over.translation: What did she call you about?

hanzi: 学习的事儿。我侄女才小学三年级,我嫂子就安排她:星期一学钢琴,星期二学画画儿,星期三学游泳,星期四学滑冰,只有星期五休息一天,星期六又叫她去上中文学校。她不喜欢学中文,所以就打电话到我这儿来,一边哭一边抱怨。
pinyin: xué xí de shìr wǒ zhí nǚ cái xiǎo xué sān nián jí wǒ sǎo zi jiù ān pái tā xīng qī yī xué gāng qín xīng qī èr xué huà huàr xīng qī sān xué yóu yǒng xīng qī sì xué huá bīng zhǐ yǒu xīng qī wǔ xiū xi yì tiān xīng qī liù yòu jiào tā qù shàng zhōng wén xué xiào tā bù xǐ huan xué zhōng wén suǒ yǐ jiù dǎ diàn huà dào wǒ zhèr lái yì biān kū yì biān bào yuàn
translation: About studying. My niece is only in the third grade, but my sister-in-law has already arranged for her: piano on Monday, painting on Tuesday, swimming on Wednesday, ice skating on Thursday. She only has Friday off, and on Saturday she has to go to Chinese school. She doesn't like to study Chinese, so she called me, crying and complaining.

scenes.scene_goal: 李哲详细解释侄女的烦恼，揭示哥嫂的教育方式。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 李哲的表情变得有些无奈和同情。他详细描述了侄女满满的课外活动日程。讲述时，插入侄女在钢琴课、画画课、游泳课等场景中疲惫不开心的快闪画面。最后回到李哲，他提到侄女是哭着打电话来抱怨的。
scenes.setting_en: Li Zhe's living room, with quick flashback inserts.
scenes.camera_movement: Static on Li Zhe, with quick cuts for flashbacks.
scenes.shot_type: Medium close-up on Li Zhe; various shots for flashbacks.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Li Zhe's expression shifts to concern. As he lists the activities, quick insert shots show the 8-year-old niece looking unhappy: sitting reluctantly at a piano, listlessly holding a paintbrush, looking tired by a swimming pool, and bored in a Chinese classroom. The scene returns to Li Zhe, who concludes by saying she called him crying and complaining.

voice_over.speaker: 李哲
voice_over.hanzi: 学习的事儿。我侄女才小学三年级,我嫂子就安排她:星期一学钢琴,星期二学画画儿,星期三学游泳,星期四学滑冰,只有星期五休息一天,星期六又叫她去上中文学校。她不喜欢学中文,所以就打电话到我这儿来,一边哭一边抱怨。
voice_over.pinyin: xué xí de shìr wǒ zhí nǚ cái xiǎo xué sān nián jí wǒ sǎo zi jiù ān pái tā xīng qī yī xué gāng qín xīng qī èr xué huà huàr xīng qī sān xué yóu yǒng xīng qī sì xué huá bīng zhǐ yǒu xīng qī wǔ xiū xi yì tiān xīng qī liù yòu jiào tā qù shàng zhōng wén xué xiào tā bù xǐ huan xué zhōng wén suǒ yǐ jiù dǎ diàn huà dào wǒ zhèr lái yì biān kū yì biān bào yuàn
voice_over.translation: About studying. My niece is only in the third grade, but my sister-in-law has already arranged for her: piano on Monday, painting on Tuesday, swimming on Wednesday, ice skating on Thursday. She only has Friday off, and on Saturday she has to go to Chinese school. She doesn't like to study Chinese, so she called me, crying and complaining.

hanzi: 没那么严重吧?她怎么说?
pinyin: méi nà me yán zhòng ba tā zěn me shuō
translation: It can't be that bad, can it? What did she say?

scenes.scene_goal: 丽莎对侄女的情况表示惊讶，并想了解更多细节。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 丽莎听完后，露出惊讶的表情，觉得这有点太夸张了，并追问侄女具体是怎么说的，为接下来的对话做铺垫。
scenes.setting_en: Li Zhe's modern, cozy living room.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Lisha looks surprised, her eyes wide. She says, 'It can't be that bad, can it?' and then asks what exactly the niece said, leaning in with concern.

voice_over.speaker: 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 没那么严重吧?她怎么说?
voice_over.pinyin: méi nà me yán zhòng ba tā zěn me shuō
voice_over.translation: It can't be that bad, can it? What did she say?

hanzi: 我不完全同意,但我能理解。
pinyin: wǒ bù wánquán tóngyì dàn wǒ néng lǐjiě
translation: I don't completely agree, but I can understand.

scenes.scene_goal: 表现丽莎对这个复杂问题的成熟看法，她既不完全赞同，也表示理解。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 丽莎轻轻搅动着咖啡，眼神中流露出思索。她微微摇头，然后看着李哲，坦诚地表达了自己复杂的感受。
scenes.setting_en: Interior of a cozy, modern cafe. Soft afternoon light. Lisa and Li Zhe are at a table.
scenes.camera_movement: Static shot, slight focus pull from the coffee cup to Lisa's face.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Close-up shot of a stylish foreign woman (Lisa) in a cafe. She gently stirs her coffee, her expression is thoughtful. She looks up at the person she's talking to, her face showing a mix of disagreement and understanding. Cinematic, soft lighting.

voice_over.speaker: 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 我不完全同意,但我能理解。
voice_over.pinyin: wǒ bù wánquán tóngyì dàn wǒ néng lǐjiě
voice_over.translation: I don't completely agree, but I can understand.

hanzi: 很多父母望子成龙,望女成凤,希望自己的孩子小时候好好儿学习,将来能做出一翻大事业。可是到现在我也想不清楚,孩子到底是将来“成龙”“成凤”好,还是有一个快乐的童年好?
pinyin: hěn duō fù mǔ wàng zǐ chéng lóng wàng nǚ chéng fèng xī wàng zì jǐ de hái zi xiǎo shí hou hǎo hāor xué xí jiāng lái néng zuò chū yī fān dà shì yè kě shì dào xiàn zài wǒ yě xiǎng bù qīng chu hái zi dào dǐ shì jiāng lái chéng lóng chéng fèng hǎo hái shì yǒu yí gè kuài lè de tóng nián hǎo
translation: Many parents hope their sons will become dragons and their daughters will become phoenixes. They hope their children will study hard when they are young so they can have a great career in the future. But even now I'm still not clear whether it's better for a child to 'become a dragon' or 'a phoenix' in the future, or to have a happy childhood.

scenes.scene_goal: 通过李哲的独白，展现中国传统教育观念与现代追求快乐童年观念的冲突，以及他个人的困惑。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 李哲听后，叹了口气，眼神飘向窗外，陷入沉思。画面中穿插几个想象中的蒙太奇镜头：一个孩子在灯下苦读，书本堆积如山（望子成龙）；另一个孩子在草地上无忧无虑地奔跑（快乐童年）。最后镜头回到李哲困惑的脸上。
scenes.setting_en: Cafe interior, with imaginative montage sequences.
scenes.camera_movement: Slow zoom-in on Li Zhe's face.
scenes.shot_type: Medium shot, with cutaways to montage.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Medium shot of a thoughtful Chinese man (Li Zhe) in a cafe. He sighs and looks out the window, lost in thought. Montage sequence: 1. A young child studying under a lamp, surrounded by stacks of books, looking tired. 2. A young child running happily in a sunny field with a kite. Cut back to Li Zhe's face, looking conflicted and confused. Cinematic, soft lighting.

voice_over.speaker: 李哲
voice_over.hanzi: 很多父母望子成龙,望女成凤,希望自己的孩子小时候好好儿学习,将来能做出一翻大事业。可是到现在我也想不清楚,孩子到底是将来“成龙”“成凤”好,还是有一个快乐的童年好?
voice_over.pinyin: hěn duō fù mǔ wàng zǐ chéng lóng wàng nǚ chéng fèng xī wàng zì jǐ de hái zi xiǎo shí hou hǎo hāor xué xí jiāng lái néng zuò chū yī fān dà shì yè kě shì dào xiàn zài wǒ yě xiǎng bù qīng chu hái zi dào dǐ shì jiāng lái chéng lóng chéng fèng hǎo hái shì yǒu yí gè kuài lè de tóng nián hǎo
voice_over.translation: Many parents hope their sons will become dragons and their daughters will become phoenixes. They hope their children will study hard when they are young so they can have a great career in the future. But even now I'm still not clear whether it's better for a child to 'become a dragon' or 'a phoenix' in the future, or to have a happy childhood.

hanzi: 我的看法是两个都重要。一个人当然应该有快乐的童年,但是长大以后也应该对社会有用,所以孩子最好还是在学校多学一些知识。
pinyin: wǒ de kànfǎ shì liǎng ge dōu zhòngyào yí ge rén dāngrán yīnggāi yǒu kuàilè de tóngnián dànshì zhǎng dà yǐhòu yě yīnggāi duì shèhuì yǒuyòng suǒyǐ háizi zuìhǎo háishi zài xuéxiào duō xué yìxiē zhīshi
translation: My opinion is that both are important. A person should of course have a happy childhood, but after growing up, they should also be useful to society. So it's best for children to learn more knowledge in school.

scenes.scene_goal: 丽莎提出一个平衡的观点，认为快乐童年和未来对社会的贡献同等重要。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 丽莎认真地听完李哲的困惑，她身体微微前倾，表情坚定而清晰地阐述自己的观点。她的手势轻微而有力，强调着“两个都重要”。
scenes.setting_en: Cafe interior. Lisa and Li Zhe are at a table.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Medium close-up on Lisa in the cafe. She leans forward slightly, listening intently, then speaks with a clear and confident expression. She makes a small, firm gesture with her hand to emphasize her point. Li Zhe is visible in the background, listening. Cinematic, warm lighting.

voice_over.speaker: 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 我的看法是两个都重要。一个人当然应该有快乐的童年,但是长大以后也应该对社会有用,所以孩子最好还是在学校多学一些知识。
voice_over.pinyin: wǒ de kànfǎ shì liǎng ge dōu zhòngyào yí ge rén dāngrán yīnggāi yǒu kuàilè de tóngnián dànshì zhǎng dà yǐhòu yě yīnggāi duì shèhuì yǒuyòng suǒyǐ háizi zuìhǎo háishi zài xuéxiào duō xué yìxiē zhīshi
voice_over.translation: My opinion is that both are important. A person should of course have a happy childhood, but after growing up, they should also be useful to society. So it's best for children to learn more knowledge in school.

hanzi: 可是你别忘了,有些知识不是从书上学的,而是从社会、从生活中学的。
pinyin: kěshì nǐ bié wàng le yǒuxiē zhīshi bú shì cóng shū shang xué de érshì cóng shèhuì cóng shēnghuó zhōng xué de
translation: But don't forget, some knowledge isn't learned from books, but from society, from life.

scenes.scene_goal: 李哲对丽莎的观点进行补充，强调生活经验和社会实践的重要性。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 李哲听了丽莎的话，赞同地点点头，但又举起一根手指，补充了自己的观点。他的表情既认真又诚恳，仿佛在分享一个重要的发现。
scenes.setting_en: Cafe interior. Li Zhe and Lisa are at a table.
scenes.camera_movement: Pan from Lisa's listening face to Li Zhe as he speaks.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Medium two-shot in a cafe. Li Zhe nods in agreement with Lisa, then raises an index finger to add a point. He speaks with a sincere and earnest expression, emphasizing his words. Lisa is listening attentively. Cinematic, warm lighting.

voice_over.speaker: 李哲
voice_over.hanzi: 可是你别忘了,有些知识不是从书上学的,而是从社会、从生活中学的。
voice_over.pinyin: kěshì nǐ bié wàng le yǒuxiē zhīshi bú shì cóng shū shang xué de érshì cóng shèhuì cóng shēnghuó zhōng xué de
voice_over.translation: But don't forget, some knowledge isn't learned from books, but from society, from life.

hanzi: 李哲,你真成了哲学家了。
pinyin: Lǐ Zhé nǐ zhēn chéng le zhéxuéjiā le
translation: Li Zhe, you've really become a philosopher.

scenes.scene_goal: 以一句轻松的玩笑结束这场深刻的对话，展现两人之间的友谊和轻松氛围。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 听到李哲富有哲理的话，丽莎忍不住笑了。她身体后仰，带着善意的玩笑口吻对李哲说。李哲也笑了，两人相视一笑，结束了对话。
scenes.setting_en: Cafe interior. Li Zhe and Lisa are at a table.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Medium two-shot in a cafe. Lisa laughs warmly after hearing Li Zhe speak. She leans back in her chair, smiling, and says something playfully. Li Zhe smiles back. They share a friendly laugh, ending the conversation on a light note. Cinematic.

voice_over.speaker: 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 李哲,你真成了哲学家了。
voice_over.pinyin: Lǐ Zhé nǐ zhēn chéng le zhéxuéjiā le
voice_over.translation: Li Zhe, you've really become a philosopher.

global_config.teaching_style: A friendly and structured approach, breaking down dialogue line by line to build foundational understanding before tackling complex grammar.
global_config.visual_style: Clean, card-based animations with clear text and highlights.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the background of Li Zhe's family, including their immigrant status and where they grew up.

narration.subtitle_en: This lesson begins by introducing Li Zhe's family. The first sentence tells us about his parents and brother. [zh:父母] means parents. They are [zh:墨西哥移民], or 'Mexican immigrants'. The sentence continues, saying he and his brother were both [zh:在加州出生、长大的], which means 'born and raised in California'. This is a common way to describe where someone is from.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 李哲的父母是墨西哥移民,他和他哥哥都是在加州出生、长大的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lǐ Zhé de fùmǔ shì Mòxīgē yímín, tā hé tā gēge dōu shì zài Jiāzhōu chūshēng, zhǎngdà de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Li Zhe's parents are Mexican immigrants. He and his older brother were both born and raised in California.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A person who has come to a different country in order to live there permanently.
The action or process of being born.
The process of developing from a child into an adult.

segments.visual_notes: Show a map highlighting Mexico and California. Use icons for 'parents' and 'brothers'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn about the brother's profession and related vocabulary.

narration.subtitle_en: Next, we learn about the brother's career. He is a [zh:电脑博士], a 'computer Ph.D.'. The word [zh:博士] means 'doctorate' or 'Ph.D.'. His job involves two main tasks: [zh:设计软件], 'designing software', and [zh:管理网站], 'managing websites'. The verb [zh:设计] means 'to design'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 哥哥是电脑博士,设计软件、管理网站。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Gēge shì diànnǎo bóshì, shèjì ruǎnjiàn, guǎnlǐ wǎngzhàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: His brother is a computer science Ph.D. who designs software and manages websites.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The highest academic degree. Can be combined with a field, like '电脑博士' (Computer Ph.D.).
The action of creating a plan or drawing for something.

segments.visual_notes: Use icons for a computer, a diploma (for Ph.D.), software, and a website.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the sister-in-law's background and learn key vocabulary.

narration.subtitle_en: Now we meet another family member, Li Zhe's [zh:嫂子], which means 'older brother's wife'. We learn she came from Hong Kong to the U.S. fifteen years ago to [zh:留学], or 'study abroad'. This is a very common verb-object word for students who go to another country for their education.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 李哲的嫂子十五年前从香港来美国留学,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lǐ Zhé de sǎozi shíwǔ nián qián cóng Xiānggǎng lái Měiguó liúxué,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Li Zhe's sister-in-law came to the U.S. from Hong Kong to study fifteen years ago,

highlight_words.explanation_en: A specific term for one's sister-in-law via their older brother.
Literally 'to remain and study'. A common term for international students.

segments.visual_notes: Show a map with an arrow from Hong Kong to the US. Use an icon for a student with a backpack.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the process of the sister-in-law settling in the US.

narration.subtitle_en: This part describes what happened after she came to the U.S. She got her [zh:硕士学位], 'master's degree'. After that, she found a job and [zh:住了下来], which means 'settled down'. The phrase [zh:住下来] implies settling in a place to live on a more permanent basis.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 拿到硕士学位以后找到工作,就在美国住了下来。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: nádào shuòshì xuéwèi yǐhòu zhǎodào gōngzuò, jiù zài Měiguó zhù le xiàlái.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: after getting her master's degree, she found a job and settled down in America.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An academic degree between a bachelor's and a doctorate.
A resultative complement indicating settling in a place to live.

segments.visual_notes: Use icons for a master's diploma, a briefcase (for work), and a house.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe a long and happy marriage.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, we learn about the couple's relationship. They have been [zh:结婚十年了], 'married for ten years'. The verb for getting married is [zh:结婚]. Their life has been [zh:很幸福], 'very happy'. [zh:幸福] is a key word for describing happiness and well-being, often in the context of life and family.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 哥哥和嫂子结婚十年了,生活一直很幸福。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Gēge hé sǎozi jiéhūn shí nián le, shēnghuó yìzhí hěn xìngfú.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: His brother and sister-in-law have been married for ten years and have had a very happy life.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The act of entering into matrimony.
A state of well-being and contentment.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a happy couple, perhaps with a '10 years' banner.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the source of conflict in the family.

narration.subtitle_en: However, a problem has emerged. The word [zh:可是] signals a turn. The issue is their daughter's [zh:教育问题], 'education issue'. Their opinions are [zh:常常不同], 'often different', and sometimes they [zh:吵得很厉害], 'argue fiercely'. The word [zh:厉害] here means 'terrible' or 'severe', describing the intensity of their arguments.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 可是最近两年在女儿的教育问题上,意见常常不同,有时吵得很厉害。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: kěshì zuìjìn liǎng nián zài nǚ'ér de jiàoyù wèntí shàng, yìjiàn chángcháng bù tóng, yǒushí chǎo de hěn lìhai.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: However, in the last two years, they have often had different opinions on the issue of their daughter's education, sometimes arguing fiercely.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The process of receiving or giving systematic instruction.
An adjective describing something as intense, severe, or formidable.

segments.visual_notes: Show two figures arguing, with a question mark over a child's head.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the immediate context of the dialogue.

narration.subtitle_en: This final sentence of the background sets the scene for the dialogue. One day, Li Zhe is on the phone with his [zh:侄女], 'niece', specifically his brother's daughter. While he's chatting, [zh:聊天儿], he sees Lisha walk in. This is the moment the main conversation begins.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 一天,李哲正在电话上跟侄女聊天儿,看见丽莎走了进来。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: yìtiān, Lǐ Zhé zhèngzài diànhuà shàng gēn zhínǚ liáotiānr, kànjiàn Lìshā zǒu le jìnlái.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: One day, Li Zhe was chatting with his niece on the phone when he saw Lisha walk in.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A specific kinship term for one's niece from their brother's side.
An informal way to say 'to chat' or 'to have a conversation'.

segments.visual_notes: Show Li Zhe on the phone, with an icon of Lisha entering the room.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn a common way to ask someone what's up.

narration.subtitle_en: Li Zhe sees Lisha and starts the conversation. He says, [zh:丽莎,有事儿吗?]. This is a very common and casual way to ask 'What's up?' or 'Is something the matter?'. [zh:有事儿吗] literally means 'Have business?' or 'Have matter?'. It's a useful phrase to check why someone has approached you.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 丽莎,有事儿吗?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lìshā, yǒu shìr ma?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Lisha, what's up?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common question to inquire about the reason for someone's presence or to ask if they need something.

segments.visual_notes: Show Li Zhe looking at Lisha with a friendly, questioning expression.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand Lisha's explanation and apology.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisha explains why she's there and apologizes for interrupting. She starts with [zh:没什么事儿], 'It's nothing important,' a direct response to his question. She then says sorry, [zh:对不起], and explains the consequence of her interruption: [zh:害得你把电话挂了], which means 'made you hang up the phone'. The phrase [zh:害得] implies causing an undesirable result.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 没什么事儿,我找天明,以为他在你这儿呢。对不起,害得你把电话挂了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: méi shénme shìr, wǒ zhǎo Tiānmíng, yǐwéi tā zài nǐ zhèr ne. duìbuqǐ, hàide nǐ bǎ diànhuà guà le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Oh, nothing important. I was looking for Tianming... Sorry to make you hang up the phone.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common way to downplay the importance of something.

highlight_words.translation: to cause trouble; to do harm to
highlight_words.explanation_en: Indicates that an action has led to an undesirable outcome for someone.

segments.visual_notes: Show Lisha with a slightly apologetic expression.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to welcome someone and explain a situation.

narration.subtitle_en: Li Zhe reassures Lisha. He starts by repeating [zh:坐], 'sit', three times. This repetition makes the invitation warm and insistent. Then he explains, [zh:刚才是我侄女给我打电话], 'That was my niece calling me just now'. He finishes by saying [zh:我们正好说完了], 'We just finished talking'. The word [zh:正好] means 'coincidentally' or 'just right', implying the timing was perfect and she didn't interrupt anything.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 坐,坐,坐。刚才是我侄女给我打电话,我们正好说完了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: zuò, zuò, zuò. gāngcái shì wǒ zhínǚ gěi wǒ dǎ diànhuà, wǒmen zhènghǎo shuō wán le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Have a seat, have a seat. That was my niece calling me. We just finished talking.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common verb and a polite invitation for a guest to take a seat.
Indicates that something happens at a very opportune or coincidental moment.

segments.visual_notes: Show Li Zhe gesturing to a chair with a welcoming smile.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask about someone's family and their age.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisha shows interest in Li Zhe's family. First, she confirms, [zh:你有侄女?], 'You have a niece?'. Then, she asks a very common question about children: [zh:今年多大了?], 'How old is she this year?'. The phrase [zh:多大了] is the standard way to ask the age of anyone who is not a very young child or a very respected elder.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你有侄女?今年多大了?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu zhínǚ? Jīnnián duō dà le?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: You have a niece? How old is she this year?

highlight_words.explanation_en: The daughter of one's brother.
A common way to ask someone's age.

segments.visual_notes: Show Lisha with a curious expression.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to state a young age using 'gang' or 'cai'.

narration.subtitle_en: Li Zhe gives a simple answer: [zh:今年刚八岁]. This means 'She just turned eight this year'. The word [zh:刚] means 'just' and emphasizes the recentness of her turning eight. You might also hear [zh:才] used in this context, like [zh:才八岁], to emphasize how young she is. Both are very common.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 今年刚八岁。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Jīnnián gāng bā suì.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: She just turned eight this year.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb indicating that an action or state has occurred a short time ago.
The measure word for age.

segments.visual_notes: Show a number '8' with a birthday cake icon.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask about the purpose of a phone call.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisha continues to inquire, asking about the reason for the call. She asks, [zh:她给你打电话有什么事啊?]. This breaks down into 'She called you' ([zh:她给你打电话]) and 'what was the matter?' ([zh:有什么事啊]). This is a natural follow-up question in a conversation.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 她给你打电话有什么事啊?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tā gěi nǐ dǎ diànhuà yǒu shénme shì a?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: What did she call you about?

highlight_words.explanation_en: The verb-object phrase for making a call.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a phone with a question mark.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the niece's situation and the use of 'cai' to mean 'only'.

narration.subtitle_en: Li Zhe begins to explain the problem. It's about [zh:学习的事儿], 'matters of studying'. He says his niece is [zh:才小学三年级], 'only in the third grade'. Here, [zh:才] is used before a number to emphasize how small it is, in this case, how young she is. Despite this, his sister-in-law, [zh:我嫂子], already [zh:安排她], 'arranges for her' a packed schedule.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我侄女才小学三年级,我嫂子就安排她:
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ zhínǚ cái xiǎoxué sān niánjí, wǒ sǎozi jiù ānpái tā:
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: My niece is only in the third grade, but my sister-in-law has already arranged for her:

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used before numbers to emphasize a small quantity or young age.
The first stage of formal education.
To make plans or preparations for an event or activity.

segments.visual_notes: Show a small child icon next to a school building. Then show a calendar icon.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary for various extracurricular activities.

narration.subtitle_en: Here is the list of activities her mother arranged. On Monday, [zh:学钢琴], 'learn piano'. On Tuesday, [zh:学画画儿], 'learn to paint'. On Wednesday, [zh:学游泳], 'learn to swim'. And on Thursday, [zh:学滑冰], 'learn to ice skate'. This list shows how busy the niece's schedule is.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 星期一学钢琴,星期二学画画儿,星期三学游泳,星期四学滑冰,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: xīngqīyī xué gāngqín, xīngqī'èr xué huàhuàr, xīngqīsān xué yóuyǒng, xīngqīsì xué huábīng,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: piano on Monday, painting on Tuesday, swimming on Wednesday, ice skating on Thursday,

highlight_words.explanation_en: A musical instrument.
The activity of painting or drawing.
The activity of swimming.
The activity of ice skating.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a calendar with icons for each activity appearing on the days of the week.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the rest of the niece's weekly schedule.

narration.subtitle_en: The schedule continues. [zh:只有星期五休息一天], 'She only has Friday off'. The word [zh:只有] means 'only'. But the break is short. On Saturday, [zh:星期六], her mom [zh:又叫她去上中文学校], 'again tells her to go to Chinese school'. The word [zh:又] means 'again', emphasizing the relentless nature of her schedule.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 只有星期五休息一天,星期六又叫她去上中文学校。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: zhǐyǒu xīngqīwǔ xiūxi yì tiān, xīngqīliù yòu jiào tā qù shàng Zhōngwén xuéxiào.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: She only has Friday off, and on Saturday she has to go to Chinese school.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to express that something is the only option or case.
To take a break from work or activity.
Indicates repetition of an action.

segments.visual_notes: Show Friday on the calendar with a 'rest' icon, then Saturday with a 'school' icon and a 'again' symbol.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the niece's feelings and the structure 'yibian... yibian...'.

narration.subtitle_en: This is the result of the packed schedule. The niece doesn't like learning Chinese, so she calls her uncle to complain. The sentence uses a useful structure: [zh:一边哭一边抱怨], which means 'crying while complaining'. The [zh:一边...一边...] pattern is used to describe two actions happening at the same time. It's a great way to paint a vivid picture.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 她不喜欢学中文,所以就打电话到我这儿来,一边哭一边抱怨。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: tā bù xǐhuan xué zhōngwén, suǒyǐ jiù dǎ diànhuà dào wǒ zhèr lái, yìbiān kū yìbiān bàoyuàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: She doesn't like to study Chinese, so she called me, crying and complaining.

highlight_words.explanation_en: To express dissatisfaction or annoyance about something.
A structure used to indicate two actions are happening simultaneously.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a child on the phone, with tear and complaint bubble icons.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express disbelief and ask for more information.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisha reacts with some skepticism. She says, [zh:没那么严重吧?]. This means 'It can't be that serious, can it?'. The phrase [zh:没那么] means 'not that...', and the final particle [zh:吧] indicates a suggestion or a guess. She then asks, [zh:她怎么说?], 'What did she say?', prompting Li Zhe to share the details.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 没那么严重吧?她怎么说?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Méi nàme yánzhòng ba? Tā zěnme shuō?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: It can't be that bad, can it? What did she say?

highlight_words.explanation_en: Describes a situation that is grave or significant.
Literally 'how did she say', but means 'what did she say'.

segments.visual_notes: Show Lisha with a slightly surprised or doubtful expression.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the niece's point of view on children's rights and parental respect.

narration.subtitle_en: Finally, Li Zhe shares his niece's surprisingly mature opinion. She said, [zh:小孩子也是人], 'children are people, too'. She believes they have their own [zh:爱好和兴趣], 'hobbies and interests'. Her conclusion is that [zh:父母应该尊重孩子自己的选择], 'parents should respect their children's own choices'. The key words here are [zh:尊重], 'to respect', and [zh:选择], 'choice'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 她说小孩子也是人,有自己的爱好和兴趣,父母应该尊重孩子自己的选择。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tā shuō xiǎoháizi yě shì rén, yǒu zìjǐ de àihào hé xìngqù, fùmǔ yīnggāi zūnzhòng háizi zìjǐ de xuǎnzé.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: She said that children are people, too. They have their own hobbies and interests. Parents should respect their children's choices.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An activity done regularly in one's leisure time for pleasure.
The feeling of wanting to know or learn about something or someone.
To have due regard for the feelings, wishes, or rights of others.
An act of selecting or making a decision when faced with two or more possibilities.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a child speaking, with a lightbulb over their head to signify a thoughtful idea.

segments.segment_title: Line 11: How can she be so articulate?
segments.teaching_goal: To understand the expression '会说话' as being articulate, not just able to speak.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisha hears the niece on the phone and is impressed, asking: [zh:她怎么这么会说话]. This translates to 'How can she be so articulate?'. Let's break it down. [zh:她] is 'she'. [zh:怎么] means 'how'. [zh:这么] means 'so' or 'this'. The key phrase here is [zh:会说话]. While it literally means 'can speak', in this context, it's a compliment meaning someone is very articulate, eloquent, or speaks sweetly. It implies the person knows how to say the right things.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 她怎么这么会说话?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tā zěnme zhème huì shuōhuà?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: How can she be so articulate?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A compliment meaning someone is very articulate or speaks in a pleasing way, beyond just the basic ability to speak.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: 会 (huì) means 'can' or 'to be good at', and 说话 (shuōhuà) means 'to speak'. Together, it implies being skilled at speaking.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a speech bubble icon next to the niece's character illustration, with sparkling effects to show 'eloquence'.

segments.segment_title: Line 12, Part 1: Learned from my brother
segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to express that something was learned from someone.

narration.subtitle_en: Li Zhe explains where his niece gets her way with words. He starts with: [zh:这些话一定是跟我哥哥学的]. This means 'She must have learned these words from my older brother.' [zh:这些话] means 'these words'. [zh:一定] means 'definitely' or 'must'. The structure [zh:跟] someone [zh:学] is very common for saying 'learn from someone'. Here, it's 'learned from my older brother'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这些话一定是跟我哥哥学的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhèxiē huà yídìng shì gēn wǒ gēge xué de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: She must have learned these words from my older brother.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb used to express certainty or conviction.
A common pattern used to indicate learning from a specific person or source.

segments.visual_notes: Show an arrow from the 'brother' character to the 'niece' character, with the text '跟...学' on the arrow.

segments.segment_title: Line 12, Part 2: Two different parenting styles
segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary related to parenting, pressure, and opposition.

narration.subtitle_en: Next, Li Zhe describes the conflict between his brother and sister-in-law. He says: [zh:我嫂子整天让她学这学那, 我哥哥反对给孩子太大的压力]. This means, 'My sister-in-law makes her study this and that all day. My brother is against putting too much pressure on the child.' [zh:嫂子] is 'older brother's wife'. [zh:整天] means 'all day long'. [zh:学这学那] is a colloquial way to say 'study a bit of everything'. The verb [zh:反对] means 'to oppose', and [zh:压力] is 'pressure'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我嫂子整天让她学这学那,我哥哥反对给孩子太大的压力,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ sǎozi zhěngtiān ràng tā xué zhè xué nà, wǒ gēge fǎnduì gěi háizi tài dà de yālì,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: My sister-in-law makes her study this and that all day. My brother is against putting too much pressure on the child.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The term for one's sister-in-law (wife of one's older brother).
A verb meaning to be against or to object to something.
Can refer to physical pressure or, as in this case, mental or emotional pressure.

segments.visual_notes: Show a split screen. On one side, the sister-in-law pointing to many books. On the other, the brother holding up a hand in a 'stop' gesture.

segments.segment_title: Line 12, Part 3: The most important thing
segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express one's opinion using '认为' and '对...来说'.

narration.subtitle_en: Li Zhe continues explaining his brother's viewpoint: [zh:他认为对孩子来说,最重要的是有一个快乐的童年]. 'He thinks that for a child, the most important thing is to have a happy childhood.' The verb [zh:认为] means 'to think' or 'to believe', used for expressing an opinion. The phrase [zh:对孩子来说] means 'as far as the child is concerned' or 'for the child'. [zh:最重要的是] means 'the most important thing is'. Finally, [zh:快乐的童年] is 'a happy childhood'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他认为对孩子来说,最重要的是有一个快乐的童年。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tā rènwéi duì háizi lái shuō, zuì zhòngyào de shì yǒu yí ge kuàilè de tóngnián.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: He thinks that for a child, the most important thing is to have a happy childhood.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to express a considered opinion or belief.

highlight_words.translation: as for..., from the perspective of...
highlight_words.explanation_en: A pattern used to specify the perspective from which a statement is made.
The period of being a child.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight '认为' and '对...来说' in different colors to show they both relate to expressing opinions from different angles.

segments.segment_title: Line 13, Part 1: A happy childhood, but...
segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to contrast a past state with a present feeling.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisha reflects on her own experience. She begins with: [zh:我的童年很快乐。可是现在,每次上中文课我就想]. This means, 'My childhood was very happy. But now, every time I have Chinese class, I think to myself...'. [zh:我的童年] is 'my childhood'. [zh:很快乐] means 'very happy'. The word [zh:可是] signals a contrast, meaning 'but' or 'however'. [zh:每次] means 'every time', setting up the current situation.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我的童年很快乐。可是现在,每次上中文课我就想,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ de tóngnián hěn kuàilè. Kěshì xiànzài, měi cì shàng Zhōngwén kè wǒ jiù xiǎng,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: My childhood was very happy. But now, every time I have Chinese class, I think to myself,

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adjective for 'happy' or 'joyful'.
A conjunction used to introduce a statement that contrasts with what has just been said.

segments.visual_notes: Show a thought bubble appearing over Lisha's head as she speaks.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a hypothetical 'if...then...' sentence structure expressing a wish about the past.

narration.subtitle_en: Here is what Lisha thinks during class: [zh:要是我小时候父母就让我学中文,我现在就轻松多了]. This is a hypothetical sentence: 'If my parents had made me learn Chinese when I was little, it would be much easier for me now.' The structure [zh:要是]...[zh:就]... means 'if...then...'. [zh:小时候] means 'in childhood'. The key feeling is [zh:轻松多了], which means 'much more relaxed' or 'much easier'. This expresses a kind of regret about not starting earlier.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 要是我小时候父母就让我学中文,我现在就轻松多了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yàoshi wǒ xiǎoshíhou fùmǔ jiù ràng wǒ xué Zhōngwén, wǒ xiànzài jiù qīngsōng duō le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: If my parents had made me learn Chinese when I was little, it would be much easier for me now.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction used to introduce a hypothetical condition.
Describes a state of being without difficulty or stress.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the words '要是' and '就' to link the cause (learning early) and effect (being easier now).

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express surprise and ask for agreement.

narration.subtitle_en: Li Zhe is surprised by Lisha's comment. He says: [zh:没想到你跟我嫂子的看法一样!你同意我嫂子的做法?]. This means, 'I didn't expect you to have the same view as my sister-in-law! Do you agree with what she's doing?'. [zh:没想到] is a great phrase for expressing surprise, literally 'didn't think of'. [zh:看法] is 'opinion' or 'view'. [zh:一样] means 'the same'. He then asks directly: [zh:你同意], 'do you agree', with her [zh:做法], her 'way of doing things'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 没想到你跟我嫂子的看法一样!你同意我嫂子的做法?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Méi xiǎngdào nǐ gēn wǒ sǎozi de kànfǎ yíyàng! Nǐ tóngyì wǒ sǎozi de zuòfǎ?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I didn't expect you to have the same view as my sister-in-law! Do you agree with what she's doing?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common expression used to show surprise about something.
Literally 'way of looking', this word means one's point of view.
A verb meaning to approve or accept something.
Refers to a person's actions or methods.

segments.visual_notes: Show a large exclamation mark next to '没想到'. Then show a question mark next to '同意...做法?'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express partial agreement or understanding.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisha gives a nuanced answer: [zh:我不完全同意,但我能理解]. This means, 'I don't completely agree, but I can understand.' This is a very useful phrase for discussions. [zh:不完全] means 'not completely'. So, [zh:不完全同意] is 'don't completely agree'. She follows this with [zh:但我能理解], 'but I can understand'. [zh:但] is a short form of 'but', and [zh:理解] means 'to understand' or 'to comprehend'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我不完全同意,但我能理解。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ bù wánquán tóngyì, dàn wǒ néng lǐjiě.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I don't completely agree, but I can understand.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb that means 'whole' or 'complete'.
Implies a deeper understanding of reasons or feelings, not just language.

segments.visual_notes: Show a scale, with '同意' on one side and '不完全' tipping it slightly, then show a heart and brain icon for '理解'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn two famous Chinese idioms about parents' aspirations for their children.

narration.subtitle_en: Li Zhe introduces a very important cultural concept with two idioms: [zh:望子成龙] and [zh:望女成凤]. The first, [zh:望子成龙], literally means 'to hope one's son becomes a dragon.' The second, [zh:望女成凤], means 'to hope one's daughter becomes a phoenix.' In Chinese culture, dragons and phoenixes are powerful, mythical creatures symbolizing success and greatness. So, these idioms describe the high hopes parents have for their children's future success.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 望子成龙 / 望女成凤
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: wàng zǐ chéng lóng / wàng nǚ chéng fèng
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Hope son becomes a dragon / Hope daughter becomes a phoenix
on_screen_text.notes: Idioms for having high expectations for your children's success.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An idiom expressing the hope that one's son will be very successful.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: 望(wàng) hope, 子(zǐ) son, 成(chéng) become, 龙(lóng) dragon.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An idiom expressing the hope that one's daughter will be very successful.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: 望(wàng) hope, 女(nǚ) daughter, 成(chéng) become, 凤(fèng) phoenix.

segments.visual_notes: Show an illustration of a boy transforming into a dragon, and a girl into a phoenix.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how parents' hopes are described.

narration.subtitle_en: After introducing the idioms, Li Zhe explains what they mean in practice: [zh:希望自己的孩子小时候好好儿学习,将来能做出一翻大事业]. This means, 'They hope their children will study hard when they are young so they can have a great career in the future.' [zh:希望] is 'to hope'. [zh:好好儿学习] means 'to study well and diligently'. [zh:将来] means 'in the future'. And [zh:做出一翻大事业] is a phrase that means 'to achieve a great career' or 'to do something big'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 希望自己的孩子小时候好好儿学习,将来能做出一翻大事业。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Xīwàng zìjǐ de háizi xiǎoshíhou hǎohāor xuéxí, jiānglái néng zuòchū yì fān dà shìyè.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: They hope their children will study hard when they are young so they can have a great career in the future.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb expressing a desire for something to happen.
A time word referring to the future.
Refers to one's profession or a major undertaking.

segments.visual_notes: Show a timeline graphic, with '小时候' (childhood) on the left and '将来' (future) on the right, with an arrow showing progression.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to frame a dilemma using '到底是...好,还是...好'.

narration.subtitle_en: Li Zhe then poses the central dilemma of the conversation: [zh:可是到现在我也想不清楚,孩子到底是将来“成龙”“成凤”好,还是有一个快乐的童年好?]. This is a long question. It means, 'But even now I'm still not clear, is it better for a child to 'become a dragon' or 'a phoenix' in the future, or to have a happy childhood?'. [zh:想不清楚] means 'can't think clearly'. [zh:到底] adds emphasis, like 'after all' or 'in the end'. The core structure is [zh:是 A 好, 还是 B 好?], asking 'is A better, or is B better?'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 孩子到底是将来“成龙”“成凤”好,还是有一个快乐的童年好?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Háizi dàodǐ shì jiānglái “chéng lóng” “chéng fèng” hǎo, háishi yǒu yí ge kuàilè de tóngnián hǎo?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Is it better for a child to 'become a dragon' or 'a phoenix' in the future, or to have a happy childhood?

highlight_words.explanation_en: Expresses confusion or inability to reach a conclusion.
An adverb used in questions to press for a definitive answer.
Used in questions to offer a choice between two or more options.

segments.visual_notes: Show two paths diverging. One path leads to a 'dragon/phoenix' icon, the other to a 'happy child playing' icon. Place a large question mark in the middle.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to state an opinion and say that two things are both important.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisha offers her opinion, starting with a clear statement: [zh:我的看法是两个都重要]. This means, 'My opinion is that both are important.' [zh:我的看法] is 'my opinion'. [zh:两个] means 'two things' or 'both'. The word [zh:都] means 'both' or 'all', and [zh:重要] means 'important'. This is a great way to begin expressing a balanced viewpoint.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我的看法是两个都重要。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ de kànfǎ shì liǎng ge dōu zhòngyào.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: My opinion is that both are important.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb that indicates inclusiveness, applying an action or state to all members of the preceding noun group.
An adjective meaning significant or of great value.

segments.visual_notes: Show the two paths from the previous scene merging back together.

segments.segment_title: Line 17, Part 2: Childhood and social utility
segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary for discussing personal and social responsibilities.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisha elaborates on her point: [zh:一个人当然应该有快乐的童年,但是长大以后也应该对社会有用]. 'A person should of course have a happy childhood, but after growing up, they should also be useful to society.' [zh:当然] means 'of course'. [zh:应该] means 'should'. [zh:但是] is 'but'. [zh:长大以后] means 'after growing up'. The final phrase, [zh:对社会有用], means 'to be useful to society'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 一个人当然应该有快乐的童年,但是长大以后也应该对社会有用,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yí ge rén dāngrán yīnggāi yǒu kuàilè de tóngnián, dànshì zhǎngdà yǐhòu yě yīnggāi duì shèhuì yǒuyòng,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: A person should of course have a happy childhood, but after growing up, they should also be useful to society.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb used to affirm something as natural or expected.
A modal verb indicating obligation or advisability.
Refers to the community or public as a whole.

segments.visual_notes: Show a balance scale. On one side, a happy child. On the other, an adult contributing to a community (e.g., a doctor, teacher).

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to give a concluding suggestion using '最好还是'.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisha concludes her thought with a suggestion: [zh:所以孩子最好还是在学校多学一些知识]. 'So it's best for children to learn more knowledge in school.' [zh:所以] means 'so' or 'therefore'. The phrase [zh:最好还是] means 'it's still best to...' or 'the best option is still...'. It's a way of giving advice after considering alternatives. The advice here is to [zh:多学一些知识], 'learn some more knowledge'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 所以孩子最好还是在学校多学一些知识。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Suǒyǐ háizi zuìhǎo háishi zài xuéxiào duō xué yìxiē zhīshi.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: So it's best for children to learn more knowledge in school.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction used to indicate a result or conclusion.
A phrase used to give a final, recommended course of action.
The general term for knowledge, information, and learning.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a school with plus signs and book icons floating out of it.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the '不是...而是...' structure for negation and affirmation.

narration.subtitle_en: Li Zhe offers a counterpoint: [zh:可是你别忘了,有些知识不是从书上学的,而是从社会、从生活中学的]. 'But don't forget, some knowledge isn't learned from books, but from society, from life.' [zh:别忘了] means 'don't forget'. The sentence uses the important pattern [zh:不是 A, 而是 B], which means 'it's not A, but rather B'. Here, A is 'learned from books', and B is 'learned from society and life'. This structure is used to correct a misconception and state the true situation.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 有些知识不是从书上学的,而是从社会、从生活中学的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yǒuxiē zhīshi bú shì cóng shū shang xué de, ér shì cóng shèhuì, cóng shēnghuó zhōng xué de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Some knowledge isn't learned from books, but from society, from life.
on_screen_text.notes: Pattern: 不是 A, 而是 B (It's not A, but rather B)

highlight_words.explanation_en: A structure used to negate one thing (A) and strongly affirm another (B).
Can mean 'life' in general, or 'to live'.

segments.visual_notes: Show a book icon with a red 'X' over it. Then show an arrow pointing to icons for 'society' (buildings, people) and 'life' (family, activities) with a green checkmark.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the suffix '-家' for experts and the playful tone of the sentence.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisha ends the exchange with a lighthearted tease: [zh:李哲,你真成了哲学家了]. 'Li Zhe, you've really become a philosopher.' [zh:真] means 'really'. [zh:成了] means 'have become'. The key word is [zh:哲学家], 'philosopher'. The suffix [zh:家] is added to fields of study to mean 'expert', like a painter, [zh:画家], or a scientist, [zh:科学家]. Here, Lisha is playfully saying Li Zhe's comment was very deep and philosophical.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 李哲,你真成了哲学家了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lǐ Zhé, nǐ zhēn chéng le zhéxuéjiā le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Li Zhe, you've really become a philosopher.
on_screen_text.notes: Suffix -家 (-jiā) indicates an expert in a field.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An expert in philosophy.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: 哲学 (zhéxué) is 'philosophy', and 家 (jiā) is a suffix for 'expert' or 'specialist'.

segments.visual_notes: Show an illustration of Li Zhe with a thinking pose, maybe stroking his chin, with a lightbulb or gears turning above his head.

segments.segment_title: Grammar: The Adverb 才 (cái) for Small Quantities
segments.teaching_goal: To teach students how to use the adverb 'cái' before a number to emphasize a small quantity or young age.

narration.subtitle_en: Now, let's focus on our first grammar point. In the dialogue, Li Zhe says [zh:我侄女才小学三年级], meaning 'My niece is *only* in the third grade.' Here, the adverb [zh:才] is used before a number to emphasize that the quantity is small or the age is young. It conveys a sense of 'just' or 'only'. For example, you could say [zh:我每天上网才三个小时], 'I go online for *only* three hours a day.' Or, if someone is very young to be getting married, you might exclaim, [zh:你今年才二十岁], 'You're *just* twenty years old'. This usage is different from the [zh:才] that indicates lateness. When placed before a number, it always highlights the smallness of that number.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我侄女才小学三年级...
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ zhínǚ cái xiǎoxué sān niánjí...
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: My niece is only in the third grade...
on_screen_text.notes: 才 (cái) + Number: Emphasizes a small quantity ('only' or 'just').

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb used before numbers to express a small quantity or young age.

grammar_points.pattern: 我每天上网才三个小时，时间不长。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Wǒ měitiān shàngwǎng cái sān ge xiǎoshí, shíjiān bù cháng.
grammar_points.explanation_en: I go online for only three hours a day. That's not a lot of time.

grammar_points.pattern: 现在才六点，球赛八点开始。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Xiànzài cái liù diǎn, qiúsài bā diǎn kāishǐ.
grammar_points.explanation_en: It's only six o'clock now. The ball game starts at eight.

grammar_points.pattern: 我大哥二十岁就念完博士了，我二十岁才上大学。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Wǒ dàgē èrshí suì jiù niàn wán bóshì le, wǒ èrshí suì cái shàng dàxué.
grammar_points.explanation_en: My older brother finished his doctorate at twenty, but I didn't even start college until I was twenty. (Here, the first 'cái' is implied for smallness, the second 'cái' indicates lateness).

segments.visual_notes: Animate the word '才' to pop out, with an icon like a magnifying glass over it to show it's emphasizing something small.

segments.teaching_goal: To teach the sentence structure where a 'noun + predicate' phrase acts as the subject, followed by an adjectival predicate.

narration.subtitle_en: Next, let's look at a sentence structure where an entire phrase acts as the subject. In the dialogue, Li Zhe wonders which is better, asking [zh:孩子到底是将来成龙成凤好，还是有一个快乐的童年好]. Notice how the adjective [zh:好], meaning 'good', is the predicate. The subjects are the two long phrases before it: 'a child becoming successful in the future' and 'having a happy childhood'. This is a common pattern where a whole clause serves as the subject. For instance, [zh:你现在读书最重要] means 'For you, studying is the most important thing right now.' The phrase [zh:你现在读书] acts as the subject for the adjectival predicate [zh:最重要]. Another example is [zh:我们寒假去哪儿旅行好？], 'Where is a good place for us to travel for winter break?'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 孩子...有一个快乐的童年好?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Háizi... yǒu yí ge kuàilè de tóngnián hǎo?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Is it better for a child... to have a happy childhood?
on_screen_text.notes: Structure: [Noun Phrase + Verb Phrase] (as Subject) + Adjective (as Predicate).

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adjective often used as a predicate for a phrasal subject.
A common adjective used as a predicate in this structure.

grammar_points.pattern: 我们寒假去哪儿旅行好？
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Wǒmen hánjià qù nǎr lǚxíng hǎo?
grammar_points.explanation_en: Where is a good place for us to travel during winter break?

grammar_points.pattern: 我不知道穿什么衣服合适。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Wǒ bù zhīdào chuān shénme yīfu héshì.
grammar_points.explanation_en: I don't know what clothes are suitable to wear.

grammar_points.pattern: 你现在读书最重要。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Nǐ xiànzài dúshū zuì zhòngyào.
grammar_points.explanation_en: For you, the most important thing now is to study.

segments.visual_notes: Visually bracket the subject phrase '[我们寒假去哪儿旅行]' and the predicate '好' to show the sentence parts.

segments.segment_title: Grammar: 不是...而是... (bú shì... ér shì...)
segments.teaching_goal: To teach the 'bú shì X, ér shì Y' pattern for negating one point and strongly affirming another.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's examine a very useful pattern for making contrasts: [zh:不是 X, 而是 Y]. It means 'it's not X, but rather Y'. Li Zhe uses this when he says, [zh:有些知识不是从书上学的，而是从社会、从生活中学的]. 'Some knowledge is not learned from books, but rather from society and from life.' This structure strongly negates the first part and affirms the second. It's perfect for clarifying your true meaning. For example, to correct a misunderstanding, you could say: [zh:我不是不希望孩子将来有一番大事业，而是不想给他太大的压力]. 'It's not that I don't want my child to have a great career, but rather that I don't want to put too much pressure on him.' It's a powerful way to correct a wrong assumption and provide the real reason.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 有些知识不是从书上学的,而是从社会、从生活中学的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yǒuxiē zhīshi bú shì cóng shū shang xué de, érshì cóng shèhuì, cóng shēnghuó zhōng xué de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Some knowledge isn't learned from books, but from society, from life.
on_screen_text.notes: Pattern: 不是 (bú shì) X, 而是 (ér shì) Y (It's not X, but rather Y).

highlight_words.explanation_en: The negation part of the pattern.
The affirmation part of the pattern, introducing the correct point.

grammar_points.pattern: 你这样做不是为他好, 而是给他找麻烦。

grammar_points.pattern: Q: 刚才找你的人是你的女朋友吧? A: 她不是我的女朋友, 而是我的嫂子。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Q: Gāngcái zhǎo nǐ de rén shì nǐ de nǚpéngyou ba? A: Tā bú shì wǒ de nǚpéngyou, ér shì wǒ de sǎozi.
